title: Delegation of 2,000

{{Quote|In broad strokes, our demands are that Chancellor Palpatine relinquish the emergency war powers granted to him, abolish the Sector Governance Decree, and begin cease-fire talks with Count Dooku's Separatists.|Mon Mothma|<ref name="Rebel Files" />}}
The '''Delegation of 2,000''' was a group of concerned [[senator]]s in the [[Galactic Senate]], many of whom were part of the [[Loyalist Committee]]. These two thousand individuals were concerned by the way in which [[Supreme Chancellor]] [[Darth Sidious|Sheev Palpatine]], who was secretly the [[Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Darth]] Sidious, had waged the [[Clone Wars]], along with his accumulation of executive powers and changes to the [[Galactic Constitution]]. During the [[Cantham House meetings]],<ref name="Rebel Files" /> the group created the [[Petition of 2,000]]. The petition formally protested Palpatine's actions and demanded he return to the Senate the emergency powers he had claimed following the conclusion of the war,<ref name="DBMT">{{Databank|meena-tills|Meena Tills}}</ref> which the delegation wanted to end by establishing a cease-fire.<ref name="Rebel Files" />

The petition also demanded the abrogation of the [[Sector Governance Decree]]. During one of the house meetings, one month before the [[Proclamation of the New Order|end]] of the [[Republic Era]], Amidala made it clear ending the war was a vital part of the petition because it robbed Palpatine of his excuse to create amendments. The [[Standard day|day]] after the formation of the Empire, [[Imperial Intelligence]] [[Senatorial arrests|arrested]] sixty-three senators on charges of conspiracy and treason, all of whom were part of the Delegation of 2,000.<ref name="Rebel Files" />

==Signatories==
*Senator [[Nee Alavar]]<ref name="Dawn">''[[Star Wars: Dawn of Rebellion: The Visual Guide]]''</ref>

{{Imp-stub}}

==Behind the scenes==
The Delegation of 2,000 appeared in deleted scenes of [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']]. These scenes were also featured in the film's [[Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith (novelization)|novelization]], which depicted [[Padmé Amidala]] as a key member of the group.<ref name="IIInovel">[[Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith (novelization)|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'' novelization]]</ref> The group was made [[canon]] when it was mentioned in Meena Tills' [[Databank (2014-)|Databank]] entry.<ref name="Meena Tills">{{SWE|characters|meenatills|Meena Tills}}</ref>
